well_plaza.text: A cracked stone plaza holds a hand-cranked well ringed by settlement folk waiting their turn. The rope is worn down to bare wire near the pulley. Someone scratched DRINK DEEP on the surrounding wall — someone else scratched DON'T underneath it.

choices.label: Wait your turn at the crank.

water_drawn.text: Cold brown water rises from below — unpleasant, but drinkable once you filter it through your rag. The locals nod and move aside. A woman nearby is trading sealed canteen caps for scraps of food.

well_deep.text: Dropping down into the shaft on a makeshift harness, you find a pre-war filtration canister lodged above the waterline. The settlement folk haul you up and press medicine and rations into your hands without a word.

rope_snap.text: The frayed wire gives way and the bucket plummets. The crank handle spins and catches you across the knuckles.

overexertion.text: The crank is heavier than it looks and you pull something in your shoulder. The locals take the handle away and let you drink anyway.

pinched_hand.text: A rusted gear catches two of your fingers. You free yourself but the wound bleeds freely for the rest of the day.
